title: perl/verilator_includer not run. No "__ALL.o" object file
body:
Good day,

I discovered a problem recently where running make (see commands below) on verilated SV doesn't create the "__ALL.o" object file. For example, the attached SV aims to create a 16 bit, 64 word RAM based on the NAND2TETRIS project and the problem was discovered with the following commands:
```
verilator -GDATA_WIDTH=16 -Wall -Wpedantic -cc -top RAM64 RAM64.sv
make -C obj_dir/ -f VRAM64.mk
```
I was expecting there to be the object file obj_dir/VRAM64__ALL.o to link with when compiling a test bench. It does create VRAM64__ALL.a but static linking with this fails.

After poking around a bit, I found that up to RAM32 can be verilated without issue. And looking through the make log, I picked out and ran manually this command:
```
/usr/bin/perl $PATH_TO/share/verilator/bin/verilator_includer -DVL_INCLUDE_OPT=include VRAM64.cpp  VRAM64_Register__D10.cpp  VRAM64_Register__D10__1__Slow.cpp  VRAM64_Register__D10__Slow.cpp  VRAM64__Slow.cpp  VRAM64__Syms.cpp > VRAM64__ALL.cpp
```
Followed by
```
ccache g++  -I.  -MMD -I/home/fried/.local/share/verilator/include -I/home/fried/.local/share/verilator/include/vltstd -DVM_COVERAGE=0 -DVM_SC=0 -DVM_TRACE=0 -DVM_TRACE_FST=0 -faligned-new -fcf-protection=none -Wno-bool-operation -Wno-sign-compare -Wno-uninitialized -Wno-unused-but-set-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-shadow      -std=gnu++14 -Os -c -o VRAM64__ALL.o VRAM64__ALL.cpp
```
and
```
ar -rcs VRAM64__ALL.a VRAM64__ALL.o
```
Now I have the object to link with when compiling the testbench C++.

Verilator 4.201 devel rev v4.200-81-g0d6099b2
[RAM64.sv.txt](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/6331165/RAM64.sv.txt)

Best regards,
emohohen
code_blocks: /usr/bin/perl $PATH_TO/share/verilator/bin/verilator_includer -DVL_INCLUDE_OPT=include VRAM64.cpp  VRAM64_Register__D10.cpp  VRAM64_Register__D10__1__Slow.cpp  VRAM64_Register__D10__Slow.cpp  VRAM64__Slow.cpp  VRAM64__Syms.cpp > VRAM64__ALL.cpp
